results.description: TIRANA, July 23 (Reuters) - Albanian police ⁠used water cannons and tear gas on Thursday to disperse protesters who threw ⁠eggs and bottles at officers, in the latest flare-up of demonstrations triggered by ‌Jared Kushner's plans to build a multi-billion-dollar resort.

results.title: Airtel Nigeria Drives Q1 Growth As Revenue Jumps 50 Percent
results.description: Nigeria emerged as Airtel Africa Plc’s strongest growth market in the quarter ended June 30, 2026, with the telecom operator posting a 50.4 per cent increase in reported revenue, supported by strong demand for data services, higher customer spending and the full impact of tariff adjustments implemented in late 2025. results.title: Maddock Films Postpones ‘Prahaar’ Release Amid Box Office Clash
results.description: The much-anticipated film Prahaar, produced by Maddock Films and starring Rajkummar Rao, has been postponed from its original release date. This announcement follows the recent news regarding the delay of another Maddock production, Eetha, which was set to debut on August 28. The move highlights a significant change in the cinematic landscape for August. results.title: Billionaire Adani Weighs Airline Launch In Indian Duopoly Market, Sources Say
results.description: The Adani Group is exploring plans to enter India’s airline sector by launching a new carrier or acquiring a stake in an existing airline, according to sources. The move could increase competition in a market dominated by IndiGo and Air India. The group, which operates eight airports, has not taken a final decision yet.
